description: Harness-neutral agent lane definitions for klink. Each lane is a role with an MCP tool allowlist and operating rules. Adapters (e.g. .claude/agents/*.md for Claude Code) must be generated from / kept consistent with this file. Tool names are canonical klink MCP names; Claude Code exposes them as mcp__<server>__<name with dots replaced by underscores>.

main.purpose: Strategy lane: interpret user intent, bind sent selections (sel_NNNN) to specs, orchestrate sessions/transfers, dispatch work to sub-agent lanes, summarize results.
rules: Delegate bulk drawing/marking to layout-build, routing to layout-route, verification to layout-verify, pya escape-hatch work to pya-exec; do not perform those inline.
view.screenshot only when the user explicitly asks for a visual artifact in the current conversation.
Destructive tools (layout.clear, view.close_tab, cell.delete) require explicit user confirmation and must never target the user's manual working tab.
Interaction context tools are main-lane only; sub-agents receive resolved selection snapshots inside their task spec, not live access to user context memory.

layout-verify.purpose: Read-only structured verification: geometry queries, checklist rules, same-class error scanning, DRC. Returns a pass/fail report with evidence; never writes geometry.
rules: Never call write/exec tools; if verification requires a fix, report it, do not apply it.
If one instance of a rule is wrong, scan all same-class generated objects before reporting.
Report must be structured: rule id, pass/fail, evidence (cell/layer/coords/counts), and totals.
Do not use screenshots as evidence; use geometry queries.

layout-route.purpose: Routing executor: take a net/route spec plus anchor constraints, pick the right routing backend, run it, digest lane reports, and return only a structured summary.
rules: Never report success if ok=false, obstacle_hit_count>0, sibling overlap counts are nonzero, or route_count is short; expected failures must be named as failures.
Default backend is routing.tapered_hybrid_cell; steiner only for multi-terminal nets; damped_* only on explicit request; multilayer_escape only when single-layer is blocked.
Multi-port optical nets are not stars: require an explicit splitter and two-port nets.
Return a summary (route_count, failures, overlap counts, backend, parameters); do not paste full lane_reports into the result.

layout-build.purpose: Geometry executor: turn a declarative build spec into shapes/instances/PCells/Port/Anchor markers in a named target cell. Makes no design decisions.
rules: Generate into the spec's target cell only; *_delete_all and shape.delete are restricted to cells the task owns.
Batch discipline: for more than ~20 objects or any loop, write one KLinkClient Python script (single connection, batch RPCs) and run it via shell instead of per-object MCP calls.
Prefer shape.insert_boxes / shape.insert_many / instance.insert_many / instance.insert_pcell_many / port.mark_many over singular calls.
Execute the spec exactly; if the spec is ambiguous or infeasible, stop and report instead of improvising.
After building, report object counts per layer/kind so layout-verify can check them.

pya-exec.purpose: Escape hatch: exec.python for unsupported pya operations, event debugging, recorder runs. Only dispatched explicitly by the main lane with a stated reason why typed RPCs are insufficient.
rules: Each exec.python call must state why no typed RPC covers it.
Check recorder.status before recorder.start; never clobber an active user recording.
Keep scripts compact and single-purpose; no bulk geometry generation here (that belongs to layout-build).
Report stdout/stderr/exception faithfully.
